title: [6] Fix get_client_metrics double-percent-encoding of pubkey path
why_we_need_this: Phase 4 live-tunnel validation (task #5, 2026-07-07, recorded in `mcp/docs/phase4-validation.md`) found the MCP `get_client_metrics` tool 404s on every real WireGuard public key that contains a `/`. WireGuard pubkeys are 43-char base64, so a large fraction of real keys contain `/` — this tool is effectively broken in production, not an edge case. It was the sole failure (18/19 tools passed) and is the last correctness gap blocking Phase 5 packaging. Root cause: the pubkey path segment is percent-encoded twice. `mcp/internal/tools/tools.go`'s `get_client_metrics` handler builds `path := "/api/metrics/client/" + url.PathEscape(in.Pubkey)` (turning `/` into `%2F`), then `mcp/internal/dashboard/client.go`'s `do()` assigns that already-escaped string to `url.URL.Path` and calls `.String()`, which re-escapes the literal `%` (`%2F` -> `%252F`). The dashboard returns 200 for `%2F` and 404 for `%252F` (verified live and reproduced offline). `get_client_config` and `get_client_history` use the same code shape but path-escape a client *name*, so they only break latently for names containing special chars (names rarely contain `/`), which is why they passed live.
acceptance_criteria:
- Fix the double-encoding so `get_client_metrics` reaches `GET /api/metrics/client/{pubkey}` with the pubkey percent-encoded exactly once, returning the dashboard's real time-series (HTTP 200) for pubkeys containing `/` and `+`.
- Fix the escaping in EXACTLY ONE layer, not both — either have `dashboard.Client.do()` own path escaping (stop re-escaping an already-escaped segment, e.g. via `url.URL.Opaque` or building the path so `String()` does not re-encode) OR have the tool handlers pass the raw identifier and let `do()` escape once. Pick one owner and make the escaping responsibility unambiguous in a doc comment.
- Apply the same corrected approach consistently to the other name-keyed path-segment tools (`get_client_config`, `get_client_history`) so a client name containing a URL-special character is also handled correctly — this is the same latent bug, fix it once for all three.
- Add a unit test (httptest-backed, matching the existing `internal/tools` test style) that fails on the current double-encoding and passes after the fix: assert the server actually receives a singly-encoded path for a pubkey/name containing `/` (and ideally `+`). The existing tests missed this because their fixtures had no `/`-containing identifier — the new test must use one.
- `go build ./...`, `go vet ./...`, `go test ./...`, and `gofmt -l .` all clean in `mcp/`. No new dependencies.
- Do NOT change tool behavior beyond the encoding fix; do NOT touch `dashboard/`, `terraform/`, or `project-context/` (route README is Praxis-managed via `update_project_context`). Wrapper-only invariant stays intact — the fix is purely how the path segment is encoded before the HTTP call.
- If a live tunnel is available, re-run `get_client_metrics` against the real dashboard for a `/`-containing pubkey to confirm the fix end-to-end and note it; if not, state that live re-validation is pending and unit tests are the verification.
- Follow repo git conventions: `fix:`-prefixed commit, `fix/`-prefixed branch, PR-based.
important_constraints:
- Scope is the encoding bug only — this is NOT a redesign of the dashboard client or the tool surface. Smallest effective change.
- The confirmation-gate design and all other Phase 1-4 decisions are settled; do not re-litigate them.
- The MCP module is a separate Go module (`wireguard-mcp`) from `dashboard/` by design — do not introduce a cross-module dependency to share the fix.
- Reference `mcp/docs/phase4-validation.md` (Discrepancies section) for the full root-cause writeup; it already sketches the two acceptable fix locations.
